skills.description: To safely manage, store, and access environment variables (secrets, configurations, API keys) throughout the application lifecycle. Proper handling prevents accidental leaks, simplifies local development, and ensures production security.

skills.name: SQL Injection Prevention
skills.title: SQL Injection Prevention
skills.summary: To prevent malicious users from executing arbitrary SQL code against your database, which could lead to data breaches, data loss, or unauthorized access.
skills.description: To prevent malicious users from executing arbitrary SQL code against your database, which could lead to data breaches, data loss, or unauthorized access.

skills.name: XSS Prevention Guide
skills.title: XSS Prevention & Sanitization
skills.summary: To protect users from Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks, where malicious actors inject scripts into a web application to steal cookies, hijack sessions, or deface websites. XSS occurs when untrusted data is rendered on a page without proper validation or encoding.
skills.description: To protect users from Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks, where malicious actors inject scripts into a web application to steal cookies, hijack sessions, or deface websites. XSS occurs when untrusted data is rendered on a page without proper validation or encoding.

skills.name: API Integration Testing (Supertest)
skills.title: API Integration Testing (Supertest + Jest)
skills.summary: To test the entire lifecycle of an HTTP request through your backend application (routing, middleware, controllers, and database interactions) to ensure that the different components work together correctly.
skills.description: To test the entire lifecycle of an HTTP request through your backend application (routing, middleware, controllers, and database interactions) to ensure that the different components work together correctly.

skills.name: Automated Accessibility Testing (Axe-core)
skills.title: Automated Accessibility Testing (Axe-core)
skills.summary: To automatically detect web accessibility (a11y) violations—such as missing ARIA attributes, poor color contrast, or missing alt text—during End-to-End (E2E) testing using Deque's [axe-core](https://github.com/dequelabs/axe-core) engine integrated with testing frameworks like Playwright or Cypress.
skills.description: To automatically detect web accessibility (a11y) violations—such as missing ARIA attributes, poor color contrast, or missing alt text—during End-to-End (E2E) testing using Deque's [axe-core](https://github.com/dequelabs/axe-core) engine integrated with testing frameworks like Playwright or Cypress.

skills.name: Component Testing (RTL)
skills.title: Component Testing (React Testing Library)
skills.summary: To test UI components as a user would experience them, focusing on the DOM structure and interactions (clicks, input typing, modal visibility) rather than the internal state or implementation details of the component.
skills.description: To test UI components as a user would experience them, focusing on the DOM structure and interactions (clicks, input typing, modal visibility) rather than the internal state or implementation details of the component.

skills.name: Contract Testing (Pact)
skills.title: Consumer-Driven Contract Testing (Pact)
skills.summary: To verify that different microservices (or frontend and backend) can communicate correctly without having to spin up both services simultaneously for slow and brittle End-to-End (E2E) tests. Contract testing ensures the Provider meets the exact data needs (the "Contract") of the Consumer.
skills.description: To verify that different microservices (or frontend and backend) can communicate correctly without having to spin up both services simultaneously for slow and brittle End-to-End (E2E) tests. Contract testing ensures the Provider meets the exact data needs (the "Contract") of the Consumer.

skills.name: E2E Testing Playwright
skills.title: E2E Testing (Cypress/Playwright)
skills.summary: To simulate real user scenarios from start to finish, ensuring that the integrated system (Frontend + Backend + Database) works as expected.
skills.description: To simulate real user scenarios from start to finish, ensuring that the integrated system (Frontend + Backend + Database) works as expected.

skills.name: Load & Performance Testing (k6)
skills.title: Load & Performance Testing (k6)
skills.summary: To evaluate how a system performs under stress and high traffic, ensuring it can handle expected and peak loads without degrading performance or crashing. [k6](https://k6.io/) by Grafana is a modern, developer-centric load testing tool written in Go, using JavaScript for test scripts.
skills.description: To evaluate how a system performs under stress and high traffic, ensuring it can handle expected and peak loads without degrading performance or crashing. [k6](https://k6.io/) by Grafana is a modern, developer-centric load testing tool written in Go, using JavaScript for test scripts.

skills.name: Mocking External Services (Jest)
skills.title: Mocking External Services (Jest)
skills.summary: To isolate the unit under test by replacing real external dependencies (e.g., APIs, databases, third-party libraries) with mock objects. This ensures that tests are fast, predictable, and don't require real network or database connections.
skills.description: To isolate the unit under test by replacing real external dependencies (e.g., APIs, databases, third-party libraries) with mock objects. This ensures that tests are fast, predictable, and don't require real network or database connections.

skills.name: Mutation Testing (Stryker)
skills.title: Mutation Testing (Stryker)
skills.summary: To measure the true effectiveness of your unit tests. Code coverage only tells you which lines were executed during a test; mutation testing tells you if your tests actually catch bugs when the code changes. [Stryker Mutator](https://stryker-mutator.io/) automatically introduces small changes (mutants) into your code (e.g., changing `+` to `-`, `true` to `false`) and runs your tests. If the tests still pass, the mutant "survived" (bad). If the tests fail, the mutant was "killed" (good).
skills.description: To measure the true effectiveness of your unit tests. Code coverage only tells you which lines were executed during a test; mutation testing tells you if your tests actually catch bugs when the code changes. [Stryker Mutator](https://stryker-mutator.io/) automatically introduces small changes (mutants) into your code (e.g., changing `+` to `-`, `true` to `false`) and runs your tests. If the tests still pass, the mutant "survived" (bad). If the tests fail, the mutant was "killed" (good).

skills.name: Snapshot Testing (Jest)
skills.title: Snapshot Testing (Jest)
skills.summary: To ensure that your UI components or data structures don't change unexpectedly. Snapshot testing records the rendered output of a component and compares it against a saved "baseline" version in future test runs.
skills.description: To ensure that your UI components or data structures don't change unexpectedly. Snapshot testing records the rendered output of a component and compares it against a saved "baseline" version in future test runs.

skills.name: Test Coverage & Quality (Istanbul)
skills.title: Test Coverage & Quality (Istanbul)
skills.summary: To measure how much of your source code is actually executed when your tests run. Test coverage reports identify "dark zones"—untested branches, functions, or lines—helping you prioritize where to add new tests and reduce the risk of hidden bugs.
skills.description: To measure how much of your source code is actually executed when your tests run. Test coverage reports identify "dark zones"—untested branches, functions, or lines—helping you prioritize where to add new tests and reduce the risk of hidden bugs.

skills.name: Test-Driven Development (TDD)
skills.title: Test-Driven Development (TDD)
skills.summary: To write higher-quality, more reliable, and better-designed code by writing tests *before* the implementation. This forces developers to think about the requirements, edge cases, and API design upfront.
skills.description: To write higher-quality, more reliable, and better-designed code by writing tests *before* the implementation. This forces developers to think about the requirements, edge cases, and API design upfront.

skills.name: Unit Test Generation Jest
skills.title: Unit Test Generation (Jest)
skills.summary: To create robust unit tests for individual functions, classes, or modules to ensure they behave as expected in isolation.
skills.description: To create robust unit tests for individual functions, classes, or modules to ensure they behave as expected in isolation.

skills.name: Visual Regression Testing (Playwright)
skills.title: Visual Regression Testing (Playwright)
skills.summary: To automatically detect unintended visual changes in a web application's UI by comparing screenshots of the current build against baseline images (snapshots) approved in a previous version.
skills.description: To automatically detect unintended visual changes in a web application's UI by comparing screenshots of the current build against baseline images (snapshots) approved in a previous version.
